title: Multi-Actor Workflow, State Machines, and Auditability · Evidence Triage
summary: A preserved decision record for multi-actor workflow, state machines, and auditability contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized auditable workflow model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.
Bind each transition to authenticated role, contextual authorization, separation of duties, and appeal or override evidence.
Specify optimistic, pessimistic, serialized, or conflict-resolution behavior when actors act on stale state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table
steps.instruction:
debug case — Two actors approve and cancel stale copies of one request while a support override bypasses the mutable status field and erases history. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.

Inspect the State Transition Table trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table. For Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table, keep State Transition Table connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For State Transition Table, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table, name the types, ownership, and stop path, State Transition Table precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Two actors approve and cancel stale copies of one request while a support override bypasses the mutable status field and erases history. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug State Transition Table, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A list of status values defines workflow behavior.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define states, events, guards, actors, effects, notifications, terminal conditions, and invalid transitions in an executable table. Artifact under review: authorized auditable workflow model.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies State Transition Table, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Workflow Recovery

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Recover interrupted, duplicated, partially completed, or disputed transitions without fabricating history.
